stimulus.content:
Read the passage below:

The President of the United States leads the executive branch. The President signs bills into law, commands the military, and works with leaders of other countries.

stem: Based on the passage, what is one main job of the executive branch?

choices.text: Writing new bills for Congress to vote on
Carrying out the laws of the country
Deciding if laws follow the Constitution
Choosing which cases go to trial

answer_explanation: The executive branch, led by the President, carries out and enforces the laws. Writing bills is the job of Congress. Deciding if laws are constitutional is done by the judicial branch. Choosing cases for trial is also a court function.
